channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Plan to Disarm Hamas in Gaza Is Detailed at U.N.
item.description: A Board of Peace member said the most dangerous weapons would be collected first. He linked compliance with disarmament to reconstruction beginning in the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hamas fighters in Gaza City in November.

item.title: 2,000 U.S. Troops From 82nd Airborne Division to Be Sent to Middle East
item.description: The order gives President Trump more military options as he considers diplomacy with Iran. It is unclear where the soldiers will go in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Members of the 82nd Airborne Division in North Carolina in 2021. It is unclear where roughly 2,000 soldiers from the division will go in the Middle East, but the location would be within striking distance of Iran, officials said.

item.title: Iran Says ‘Non-Hostile’ Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships in the Gulf, near the Strait of Hormuz, seen from the United Arab Emirates earlier this month.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The U.S. was said to have sent Iran a peace plan via Pakistan on a day that the Iranians fired a torrent of missiles across the region.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Emergency workers inspecting the site of a strike in a residential area of the northwestern city of Tabriz, Iran, on Tuesday.

item.title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Halt Iran War Without Authorization
item.description: Senate Democrats failed for the third time to advance a resolution that would force the removal of troops from hostilities in Iran unless Congress approves offensive operations.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Christopher S. Murphy, Democrat of Connecticut, introduced Tuesday’s war powers resolution.

item.title: Iran Signals Resilience With Volley of Missiles Across Middle East
item.description: The barrage continued as officials said the U.S. had sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the war. But Iranian officials have publicly denied that Washington and Tehran are talking directly.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Gabby Sobelman, Erica L. Green and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gathering around a fragment of a missile that landed in an orchard near the Palestinian village of Hares in the West Bank on Tuesday.

item.title: Pakistan Offers to Host Talks Between Iran and the United States
item.description: Pakistan, which has cultivated ties with both Washington and Tehran, sees a diplomatic opening to intervene in the war in Iran, its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, right, with President Trump during last year’s Gaza peace summit in Egypt.

item.title: United States Said to Have Sent Iran a Plan to End the Middle East War
item.description: The 15-point plan was delivered via Pakistan, whose army chief has emerged as the key interlocutor between the United States and Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment shattered by an airstrike in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: High Oil and Gas Prices Could Outlast Trump’s War With Iran
item.description: While the president has promised rapid relief, Americans could feel the financial sting of the conflict for some time after it ends.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Even when the conflict ends, it may be weeks or months before American families and businesses see a true break in spiraling energy costs.

item.title: Lebanon Expels Iranian Envoy as Rift With Tehran Deepens
item.description: The decision by Lebanon’s foreign ministry has heightened fears of internal instability. Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed militant group and political party, was quick to condemn the move.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Reza Shibani, Iran’s ambassador to Beirut, has been ordered to leave Lebanon by the end of the week.

item.title: Qatar Is Not Mediating U.S.-Iran Talks, Foreign Minister Says
item.description: Qatar’s Foreign Ministry warned that the regional security system has been shattered, as the war has destroyed trust between Gulf countries and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Majed al-Ansari, Qatar’s Foreign Ministry spokesman, said the country isn’t involved with mediation talks.

item.title: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz will be difficult without a deal with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Agnes Chang, Adina Renner and Samuel Granados

item.title: Why President Trump Has a Big Oil Problem
item.description: Our national security correspondent David E. Sanger looks at President Trump’s trouble handling retaliatory attacks by Iran that have largely choked off the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Coleman Lowndes, Laura Salaberry, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Alexandra Ostasiewicz and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran’s New Security Chief Is a Hard-Line Former Guards Commander
item.description: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr replaces Ali Larijani, who was killed last week in an Israeli strike. He has a history of expanding the Guards’ reach into Iran’s politics.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr in 2024.

item.title: Trump’s Threat to ‘Obliterate’ Iran’s Power Stations Could Constitute a War Crime, Rights Experts Say
item.description: Intentionally targeting the country’s energy infrastructure could constitute a war crime under international law.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gibbons-Neff and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Tehran in 2024. President Trump threatened over the weekend to attack Iran’s power plants.

item.title: Israel Plans to Control Large Parts of Southern Lebanon, Defense Minister Says
item.description: Israel Katz, the Israeli defense minister, suggested that Israeli troops might remain in parts of Lebanon even after the fighting there winds down.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Israeli military vehicles near the border of Lebanon on Saturday.

item.title: Three of the Lives Lost in One Day of Israeli Strikes in Lebanon
item.description: A 4-year-old, a professor and an aid worker are among those killed in separate airstrikes as Israel pummels towns and cities, saying it is targeting Hezbollah operatives.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: Philippines Declares National Emergency Over High Fuel Prices
item.description: The Southeast Asian country imports 90 percent of its oil from the Middle East, and the government is under intense pressure to deal with the situation.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lining up at a gas station in the Manila area this month. Transportation workers in the Philippines have called for mass protests this week over fuel prices.

item.title: War in Iran Is Disrupting Air Travel, Hitting Middle East Hardest
item.description: Some airlines and travelers, especially in the Middle East and elsewhere in Asia, are bearing the brunt of the pain, while others are doing better.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi, Pragati K.B. and Aaron Krolik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A plane taking off from Beirut-Rafik Hariri International Airport in Lebanon last week, as smoke rose from the site of an Israeli airstrike.

item.title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
item.description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump hosted Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia at the White House last year.

item.title: Wicked Stepmother No Longer, a Female Pharoah Gets a Reputational Makeover
item.description: A reassessment of damaged 3,500-year-old statuary adds to evidence that Queen Hatshepsut wasn’t the villain that scholars long took her to be.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Yeganeh Torbati, Gabby Sobelman and Erica L. Green

item.title: Ukraine Spent Big to Shield Energy Industry From Drones. Is the Mideast Next?
item.description: With the use of electronic jamming systems and interceptor drones, the Ukrainian national oil and gas company may be a model for others.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A damaged area of a Naftogaz gas facility following Russian missile and drone attacks, in an undisclosed location in Ukraine in December.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: Conflicting signals about whether any negotiations to end the fighting were in progress created confusion.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The outskirts of Beirut, Lebanon, Monday night. Airstrikes hit the southern parts of the capital known as a Hezbollah stronghold soon after Israel issued evacuation warnings.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Is Negotiating With Iran, Which Publicly Denies His Claim
item.description: “Very, very strong talks,” President Trump claimed. Fake news, replied an Iranian parliamentary leader.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Aaron Boxerman, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment building hit by a strike in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Pentagon Officials Weigh Deployment of Airborne Troops for Iran War
item.description: The combat forces would come from a brigade of about 3,000 soldiers capable of deploying anywhere in the world within 18 hours.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston for The New York Times
item.media:description: Troops from the 82nd Airborne Division deploying from Fort Bragg, N.C., to Eastern Europe in response to the war in Ukraine in 2022.

item.title: Trump Delays Energy Strikes, but Iran’s Infrastructure Is Already Battered
item.description: U.S. and Israeli attacks on power plants and other civilian infrastructure risk escalating the conflict across the region, and angering Iranians who oppose the government.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike on an oil storage facility in Tehran earlier this month.

item.title: Energy Crisis Will Not Be Resolved Quickly if War Ends, I.E.A. Chief Warns
item.description: Fatih Birol, the leader of the International Energy Agency, said the Iran war was a bigger crisis than the two oil shocks in the 1970s combined.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lukas Coch/Australian Associated Press, via Reuters
item.media:description: Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency, speaks in Canberra, Australia, on Monday.

item.title: Israel Says Its Own Artillery Killed a Farmer, Not a Hezbollah Rocket
item.description: Ofer Moskovitz, 60, an avocado farmer in a kibbutz on Israel’s border with Lebanon, was killed Sunday when the car he was riding in was hit.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ofer Moskovitz, 60, holding avocados on a dirt road near Israel’s border with Lebanon last week.

item.title: For Western Oil Companies, War in Iran Means Bigger Profits, and Risks
item.description: American and European oil and gas companies are expected to earn a lot more as prices surge but are worried about the future.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Storage tanks in Stanton, Texas. Companies that operate in places like West Texas are earning a lot more for each barrel of oil they produce.

item.title: Iran attempted to attack a far-off military base.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez

item.title: Sidelined by War With Iran, Gaza Residents Remain in Limbo
item.description: The new war has led to panic buying and a surge in food prices for Gazans as they try to recover from Israel’s two-year offensive against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Bilal Shbair, Isabel Kershner and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Farnaz Fassihi, Aaron Boxerman, Richard Pérez-Peña and Ephrat Livni

item.title: Iranians Fear Trump’s Threat to Strike Power Plants
item.description: With President Trump threatening to strike Iranian power plants, some war-weary civilians are panicking over a possible new debacle.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran residents on Sunday, when tensions were rising over whether power would be cut to the capital and home to 10 million people.

item.title: Israel’s Missile Defense Under Scrutiny After Iranian Attack
item.description: Two missiles landed hours apart, wreaking havoc in two towns near a heavily guarded nuclear site in the Negev Desert.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peering Sunday at damage where a missile struck the night before in Arad, Israel.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli military said its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon would intensify, while Iran threatened to attack civilian infrastructure if President Trump followed through with an ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Responds to Trump’s Energy Threat With Defiance and Warnings of Its Own
item.description: Tehran “will not hesitate in defending its people and its land,” a senior official said, after President Trump threatened to destroy Iranian power plants.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s first vice president, Mohammad Reza Aref, center, in February.

item.title: Israel Orders Military to Intensify Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel Katz, the defense minister, said he ordered troops to destroy more bridges and buildings in southern Lebanon, stoking worries that Israel was widening a military-controlled buffer zone there.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cars turn around on a bridge in southern Lebanon after an Israeli strike destroyed it on Sunday.

item.title: Residents in Southern Israel Are Shaken After Direct Strikes by Iranian Missiles
item.description: Attacks on Saturday injured dozens in Arad and Dimona, two cities closest to Israel’s main nuclear research facility.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency workers at the site of a missile strike in Dimona, Israel, on Sunday.

item.title: On Beirut’s Waterfront, Loss Meets Life, and Luxury, Amid War
item.description: Thousands displaced by Israeli strikes on Lebanon’s capital now shelter along the promenade hugging the Mediterranean Sea. They share it with joggers, cyclists and dog walkers, alongside dizzying displays of wealth.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Diego Ibarra Sanchez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A displaced man in Beirut, Lebanon, sitting near the sea. More than a million people have been displaced in the country since the latest conflict between Israel and Hezbollah began.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Julian Barnes and Isabel Kershner

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: An Iranian missile wounded dozens in Dimona, a city near Israel’s main nuclear research facility, as Tehran showed no signs of backing down.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Israeli soldier uses a flashlight while walking in the ruins of a damaged building in the southern Israeli city of Dimona.

item.title: Iranian missile strikes Israeli city near main nuclear research facility.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: Emergency workers approach a site where Iranian missiles struck Dimona, Israel on Saturday.

item.title: With Fire and Song, Kurds in Turkey Hail the Start of Spring
item.description: The Newroz holiday comes at a critical time, as Kurds in Turkey push for peace while their brethren in Syria and Iran fear further conflict.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Safak Timur and Rena Effendi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Kurds celebrating Newroz, an ancient holiday that marks the start of spring in the northern hemisphere, in Diyarbakir, Turkey.

item.title: Internet Blackout Keeps Iranians From Reaching Loved Ones During Nowruz Holiday
item.description: The current restrictions on the internet in Iran are coinciding with Nowruz, the observance of the Persian New Year.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shoppers in Tehran prepare for Nowruz, the Persian New Year, even as travel difficulties and an internet blackout keep families apart.

item.title: How the Litani River Shapes the Israel-Hezbollah War
item.description: The 90-mile waterway, back in focus amid Israel’s renewed offensive in Lebanon, could define how far the fighting spreads.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damage to a bridge over the Litani river in southern Lebanon after it was struck on Wednesday.

item.title: Israel Strikes Across Lebanon Amid Fierce Ground Fighting in the South
item.description: The Israeli military said it was stepping up attacks aimed at infrastructure in Beirut belonging to Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damaged buildings in the southern neighborhoods of Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, on Saturday, after an Israeli airstrike.
